PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST WITH REGARD TO ORDER FOR ARBITRATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF NAIFY, MICHAEL

DISCOVERY 612, PRESENT: STEVEN M. KIPPERMAN, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, MICHAEL NAIFY. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR THEIR DEPOSITIONS, TO BE NOTICED BY PLAINTIFFS, AND TO BE COMPLETED NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 25, 2005. THE DEPOSITION MAY BE NOTICED TO TAKE PLACE IN SAN FRANCISCO. DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO APPEAR FOR THEIR DEPOSITIONS WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDANTS JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,285.69, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

LAW AND MOTION, 301, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL NAIFY'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR INSUFFICIENT SERVICE. MOVING PARTY MAILED THE MOVING PAPERS ON 07/O1/05, ONLY 16 COURT DAYS PLUS 3 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING DATE. CCP SECTION 1005(B) REQUIRES THE MOVING PAPERS TO BE FILED AND SERVED 16 COURT DAYS PLUS 5 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING IF SERVICE IS BEING MADE VIA MAIL. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON CSR#1558
